page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores using the AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.55 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2695.88 placing it in the top 25%. Passmark memory testing also ranks in the top 25% with a score of 2870.11. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the middle 50% tier. Large block read memory bandwidth is average at 42.34 GB/sec, but large block write memory bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 18.93 GB/sec.

Given its balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU cores, the instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It achieves average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it delivers strong text generation speeds of 285.02 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models, though performance scales down to average levels for larger models. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy or memory-balanced applications that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.8xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores running at 2.55 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, both placing in the top 25% tier. However, multi-core benchmarks and large block read memory bandwidth fall in the middle 50%, while large block write memory bandwidth is a relative bottleneck in the bottom 25%. The instance is suited for balanced workloads such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.8xlarge is an x86_64 server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and 128 GB RAM for compute-intensive and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores running at 2.55 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware, utilizing 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
